title: AI vs. Dedicated Software: The Real Efficiency Debate #shorts
summary: Cyril Imhof questions whether integrating AI broadly for every task delivers long-term efficiency gains or constitutes wasteful spending. The piece challenges the assumption that AI universally outperforms dedicated software solutions and suggests drawbacks to blanket AI adoption.
writeup: This short from Cyril Imhof questions whether applying AI to every task produces real long-term efficiency gains or amounts to wasteful spending. It challenges the assumption that AI universally outperforms dedicated, task-specific software and points to drawbacks of blanket AI adoption. The framing draws on ideas including self-improving AI and cache efficiency to weigh general-purpose AI against purpose-built solutions.
